text: wrap text. For maximum legibility, label text should remain on a single line.

text: Use visually-prominent filled buttons for the most important actions

text: Place the icon to the left of the label in buttons with text in left-to-right languages

alt: Filled button with the icon to the right of the label in a right-to-left language.

text: Filled buttons can be responsive to the layout grid and help emphasize main actions

text: The tonal style has less emphasis than filled or emphasis

caption: The onboarding animations use Google colors to guide attention and create a coherent flow. Abstract metaphors help communicate complicated technical steps in a user-friendly way. Balanced imagery means success, motion cycles mean processing, and collapsed ‘blocks’ indicate a failure state.
text: The onboarding animations use Google colors to guide attention and create a coherent flow. Abstract metaphors help communicate complicated technical steps in a user-friendly way. Balanced imagery means success, motion cycles mean processing, and collapsed ‘blocks’ indicate a failure state.

caption: Several screens require people to select an item, like choosing your favorite music provider or a Wi-Fi network. The design team didn’t want to litter these screens with checkboxes, but they still needed to communicate tappability and action. The solution was to use a visual language of color and iconography to indicate selection – the icons transform into a check mark and change to blue, showing advancement through the flow.
text: Several screens require people to select an item, like choosing your favorite music provider or a Wi-Fi network. The design team didn’t want to litter these screens with checkboxes, but they still needed to communicate tappability and action. The solution was to use a visual language of color and iconography to indicate selection – the icons transform into a check mark and change to blue, showing advancement through the flow.

caption: Gmail’s conversation view includes a new, more limited range of Material elevation, using a shadow to underscore the open reply box. The reply box’s send button showcases another colorful touch – using a filled background color to highlight the main action on the page. Together, both treatments help users focus on important, action-oriented aspects of the UI.
text: Gmail’s conversation view includes a new, more limited range of Material elevation, using a shadow to underscore the open reply box. The reply box’s send button showcases another colorful touch – using a filled background color to highlight the main action on the page. Together, both treatments help users focus on important, action-oriented aspects of the UI.

caption: Updated guidance on the responsive layout grid allowed the designers to pare down layers of legacy features while introducing multiple panes of content in a collapsible, left-side navigation, as well as a new right-hand panel providing quick access to Calendar, Tasks, and other multi-tasking features.
text: Updated guidance on the responsive layout grid allowed the designers to pare down layers of legacy features while introducing multiple panes of content in a collapsible, left-side navigation, as well as a new right-hand panel providing quick access to Calendar, Tasks, and other multi-tasking features.

caption: Using a single typeface for every headline works well on mobile, where the user only sees a few articles at a time. But on the web – where more headlines are visible at once – this proves visually overwhelming. Material type guidance suggests creating a type scale of 12 fonts and text sizes to use throughout a UI. The designers of Google News were able to use their type scale’s additional headline styles to create more variation on the web while still supporting a consistent visual hierarchy across platforms.
text: Using a single typeface for every headline works well on mobile, where the user only sees a few articles at a time. But on the web – where more headlines are visible at once – this proves visually overwhelming. Material type guidance suggests creating a type scale of 12 fonts and text sizes to use throughout a UI. The designers of Google News were able to use their type scale’s additional headline styles to create more variation on the web while still supporting a consistent visual hierarchy across platforms.

caption: (1) Instead of a shallow, waved line, like in calligraphic Naskh, the top is curved and open, adding a geometric quality to the letter. (2) The curves are static using the typical Naskh structure, but treated in a controlled, geometric way.
text: (1) Instead of a shallow, waved line, like in calligraphic Naskh, the top is curved and open, adding a geometric quality to the letter. (2) The curves are static using the typical Naskh structure, but treated in a controlled, geometric way.

caption: (1) The almost constant thickness of the strokes is similar to the Latin counterpart of Readex Pro, derived from Lexend. (2) The baseline is flat and horizontal, following the Naskh magazine style, and brings the rhythm closer to the Latin. The diacritic (accent mark) is a rhombus, like in Naskh.
text: (1) The almost constant thickness of the strokes is similar to the Latin counterpart of Readex Pro, derived from Lexend. (2) The baseline is flat and horizontal, following the Naskh magazine style, and brings the rhythm closer to the Latin. The diacritic (accent mark) is a rhombus, like in Naskh.

text: Avoid significantly altering the shape of a snackbar container

The large initial size indicates that longer responses are possible and encouraged. These should be used instead of multi-line fields on the web. Ensure the height of a text area fits within mobile screen sizes.

caption: Claude Monet’s “Impression, Sunrise” shows why contrast measures legibility for all, not just non-standard vision: the sun and sky have exactly the same luminance, creating a hazy effect that makes the sun hard to focus on. When viewing the image in black and white, only seeing luminance, the sun disappears completely.
text: Claude Monet’s “Impression, Sunrise” shows why contrast measures legibility for all, not just non-standard vision: the sun and sky have exactly the same luminance, creating a hazy effect that makes the sun hard to focus on. When viewing the image in black and white, only seeing luminance, the sun disappears completely.

caption: Use the default badge color
text: Use the default badge color

alt: Small and large badges in custom colors on 3 navigation items.
caption: Avoid using custom color roles for the badge container and label text. If custom roles are necessary, make sure they have contrast of at least 3:1.
text: Avoid using custom color roles for the badge container and label text. If custom roles are necessary, make sure they have contrast of at least 3:1.

alt: 4 icons with increasing number badges. The badges represent quantities, using a "+" symbol for quantities over 999.
caption: Large badges with one to four characters
text: Large badges with one to four characters

alt: 4-digit numbers condensed to a 3-digit badge with "+" to fit the badge container's width.
caption: Truncate badge labels as needed
text: Truncate badge labels as needed

alt: 4-digit and 5-digit number badges on navigation items exceed the badge container's width and get cut off at the edge.
caption: let the badge get cut off or collide with another element
text: let the badge get cut off or collide with another element

alt: Large badge to the right of a navigation rail item.
caption: Use a large badge to show count information when visual collisions aren’t an issue, such as in a navigation rail
text: Use a large badge to show count information when visual collisions aren’t an issue, such as in a navigation rail

alt: Small badge on an icon button in an app bar.
caption: Use a small badge when spaces are tightly constrained, such as app bars. Small badges won’t run into the edge of the screen.
text: Use a small badge when spaces are tightly constrained, such as app bars. Small badges won’t run into the edge of the screen.

alt: Large badge placed at the end of a tab.
caption: When an icon with a badge is followed by text or another element, place a large badge at the trailing edge
text: When an icon with a badge is followed by text or another element, place a large badge at the trailing edge

alt: Large badge overlapping the icon and text in a tab.
caption: Avoid using a large badge when it might overlap with a trailing element. Either place it at the trailing edge or use a small badge instead.
text: Avoid using a large badge when it might overlap with a trailing element. Either place it at the trailing edge or use a small badge instead.

text: Large badge

text: In navigation bars, hide the badge once the destination has been selected

alt: Small and large badges on 2 icon buttons.
caption: Small badgeLarge badge containerLarge badge label
text: Small badgeLarge badge containerLarge badge label

alt: A small badge on a navigation item.

text: A small badge uses only shape to indicate a status change or new notification

alt: Number 10 displayed within large badge on a navigation item.

text: A large badge displays a number within a container to indicate a quantifiable status change related to a destination

alt: Small and large badges on the left side of 2 navigation items in a right-to-left language.

text: Change the position of the badge for right-to-left languages

alt: Small and large badges at random positions on 3 icon buttons on a navigation rail.
caption: Badges have fixed positions. Don’t change the position of the badge arbitrarily or place the badge over the icon.

alt: A dialog in a UI screen requesting user confirmation to discard calendar event
caption: Use native elements, such as the standard platform dialog
text: Use native elements, such as the standard platform dialog

alt: A banner requesting user confirmation to discard a calendar event
caption: Be wary of using non-standard elements, such as a non-standard platform dialog to perform a standard dialog task. It requires extra testing to work well with assistive technology.
text: Be wary of using non-standard elements, such as a non-standard platform dialog to perform a standard dialog task. It requires extra testing to work well with assistive technology.

alt: Podcast is selected and exited quickly on the left, while a podcast is selected and exited more slowly on the right.
caption: Transitions with a default motion setting Transitions with a reduced motion setting turned on
text: Transitions with a default motion setting Transitions with a reduced motion setting turned on

alt: Selected restaurant information fades out as it shrinks to reveal Google Map beneath..
caption: Fade content out before fading new content in to maintain a clean design
text: Fade content out before fading new content in to maintain a clean design

alt: Restaurant information remains visible as it shrinks and reveals the map beneath.
caption: Avoid showing cross faded content, the overlap of partially transparent elements can result in messy and distracting frames
text: Avoid showing cross faded content, the overlap of partially transparent elements can result in messy and distracting frames

alt: Message window remains transparent with details visible as it shrinks causing text beneath to become obscured.
caption: fade a bottom sheet as it enters and exits, it creates distracting cross faded frames
text: fade a bottom sheet as it enters and exits, it creates distracting cross faded frames

alt: Calendar event expands evenly and quickly comes to a rest.
caption: Transitions should have a simple style
text: Transitions should have a simple style

alt: Calendar event expands with window bouncing momentarily and bouncing again on collapse.
caption: Common transitions should not use overt style effects like bouncy springs
text: Common transitions should not use overt style effects like bouncy springs

alt: Image within a carousel is selected and expands to fill the screen.
caption: A container transform creates a clear connection between the thumbnail and expanded image. It also makes this hero transition more expressive.
text: A container transform creates a clear connection between the thumbnail and expanded image. It also makes this hero transition more expressive.

alt: Battery life menu item among many setting options is selected and expanded to fill the screen.
caption: use container transform in apps with deep hierarchies, the motion becomes excessive. The expressive style also doesn't fit this utility focused navigation.
text: use container transform in apps with deep hierarchies, the motion becomes excessive. The expressive style also doesn't fit this utility focused navigation.

alt: A One-Year-Ago photo memory is selected and expanded to take over the screen.
caption: use forward and backward transitions on hero moments like opening a photo memory
text: use forward and backward transitions on hero moments like opening a photo memory

alt: Individual message is selected with simple motion, and moves back with simple motion.
caption: Platform default forward and backward transitions are a sensible choice for common navigation
text: Platform default forward and backward transitions are a sensible choice for common navigation

alt: Individual message is selected, causing a more radical take-over of the screen.
caption: Container transform transitions require custom implementations and the motion may feel excessive when used frequently
text: Container transform transitions require custom implementations and the motion may feel excessive when used frequently

alt: Multiple apps move in tandem with the same motion effect, appearing in sync.
caption: These four Android apps use the same forward and backward transition, making them feel like a cohesive family of apps
text: These four Android apps use the same forward and backward transition, making them feel like a cohesive family of apps

alt: Tabs in a music app shift horizontally without interruption when selected.
caption: A tab component uses a lateral transition type
text: A tab component uses a lateral transition type

alt: Tabs in a music app fade as they rotate horizontally.
caption: Fading content as it slides makes the peer relationship and swipe gesture less obvious. The style also may be confused with a forward and backward transition.
text: Fading content as it slides makes the peer relationship and swipe gesture less obvious. The style also may be confused with a forward and backward transition.

alt: Message selected within messaging app moves laterally.
caption: A lateral transition should not be used for common forward and backward navigation as it results in an excessive amount of motion
text: A lateral transition should not be used for common forward and backward navigation as it results in an excessive amount of motion

alt: Navigation between home and music app occurs with a quick fade, suggesting that items in each are distinct.
caption: A top level transition type is used with a navigation bar, rail and drawer
text: A top level transition type is used with a navigation bar, rail and drawer

alt: Navigation between home a music app occurs in a seamless lateral scroll, falsely suggesting that items in each are connected.
caption: use a lateral transition to move between top level destinations. The gesture conflicts with carousel and list item gestures.
text: use a lateral transition to move between top level destinations. The gesture conflicts with carousel and list item gestures.

alt: Animation showing a standard bottom sheet using the enter and exit transition over a map.
caption: This bottom sheet uses an enter and exit transition pattern
text: This bottom sheet uses an enter and exit transition pattern

alt: Animation showing a card expanding to a full screen on click and using the enter and exit transition.
caption: use an enter and exit pattern for navigating hierarchical screens
text: use an enter and exit pattern for navigating hierarchical screens

alt: Podcast window opens with pulsing outline that frames content added slowly.
caption: Transitions should use skeleton loaders with a subtle pulsing animation to stabilize a layout as it loads
text: Transitions should use skeleton loaders with a subtle pulsing animation to stabilize a layout as it loads

alt: Podcast window opens with no outline, and content added into haphazard clusters.
caption: Content should not pop in and shift locations during a transition
text: Content should not pop in and shift locations during a transition

alt: Items in a music library are smoothly transitioned between.
caption: Animated transitions help users orient themselves as they navigate
text: Animated transitions help users orient themselves as they navigate

alt: Items in a library are selected with stark, quick transitions.
caption: For most common transitions, jump cuts are jarring and disorienting
text: For most common transitions, jump cuts are jarring and disorienting

alt: An image within a horizontal carousel is also scrolled horizontally.
caption: These carousel transitions have a coherent spatial layout while navigating between a collapsed and expanded view
text: These carousel transitions have a coherent spatial layout while navigating between a collapsed and expanded view

alt: An image within a horizontal carousel is scrolled vertically.
caption: Switching between horizontal and vertical carousel layouts creates a confusing spatial model
text: Switching between horizontal and vertical carousel layouts creates a confusing spatial model

alt: Selected song expands in a cohesive upward direction, marked by two blue lines showing unified movement.
caption: This transition has a simple vertical motion that’s easy to follow
text: This transition has a simple vertical motion that’s easy to follow

alt: Selected song expands in a jarring manner with multiple elements moving in different directions, marked by multiple red lines.
caption: animate many persistent elements independently. The various moving parts are distracting
text: animate many persistent elements independently. The various moving parts are distracting

caption: Dynamic color adapts to each person and upholds the Material You ethos of respecting the user.
text: Dynamic color adapts to each person and upholds the Material You ethos of respecting the user.

caption: ‘Semantic’ colors express conventional meaning, and intuitively represent concepts like ‘stop’.
text: ‘Semantic’ colors express conventional meaning, and intuitively represent concepts like ‘stop’.

caption: Dynamic color schemes in Material are algorithmic (generated from a set of rules) and derived from the user’s wallpaper.
text: Dynamic color schemes in Material are algorithmic (generated from a set of rules) and derived from the user’s wallpaper.

caption: In a smart home app, specific semantic colors could be used to represent concepts like lighting and heating, which could clash with the user color scheme.
text: In a smart home app, specific semantic colors could be used to represent concepts like lighting and heating, which could clash with the user color scheme.

caption: Replacing semantic colors with the user scheme looked pleasing, but made apps that relied on semantic color harder to use.
text: Replacing semantic colors with the user scheme looked pleasing, but made apps that relied on semantic color harder to use.

caption: The design team needed to maintain different semantic colors while creating harmony in the color scheme overall.
text: The design team needed to maintain different semantic colors while creating harmony in the color scheme overall.

caption: A color can be described by its hue, tone, and chroma.
text: A color can be described by its hue, tone, and chroma.

caption: A particular hue of a color can be considered warmer or cooler, depending on where it is on the spectrum.
text: A particular hue of a color can be considered warmer or cooler, depending on where it is on the spectrum.

caption: Sets of colors that sit closer together on the hue spectrum can appear more harmonious than those farther apart.
text: Sets of colors that sit closer together on the hue spectrum can appear more harmonious than those farther apart.

caption: Material creates color harmony by adjusting custom colors towards the user color for a more pleasing overall scheme.
text: Material creates color harmony by adjusting custom colors towards the user color for a more pleasing overall scheme.

alt: A mockup of grid columns, showing compact, medium/expanded, and large/extra-large breakpoints.
caption: The number and size of columns changes based on breakpoints
text: The number and size of columns changes based on breakpoints

alt: Carousel and text resizing to align with content rulers, creating a structured layout.
caption: Realigning primary components or content to a content ruler can create strong hierarchy and visual rhythm across a product
text: Realigning primary components or content to a content ruler can create strong hierarchy and visual rhythm across a product

alt: A switch’s unselected handle icon is a moon and the selected state icon is a pencil.

text: The lowercase “j” has an exaggerated tail, the uppercase “I” has serifs. The lowercase “i” has a spur on the top left of the stem. The lowercase “l” has a curved terminal at the bottom right. There is more space than usual between the bottom of the exclamation mark and the dot.

text: prompt the user with a notification or email that might interrupt an important moment

text: Make it easy for users to start or stop receiving notifications

text: bury next steps

text: be so generic as to lose value

alt: Diagram showing the 5 elevation levels and their respective dp values.
caption: Material uses six levels of elevation, each with a corresponding dp value. These values are named for their relative distance above the UI’s surface: 0, +1, +2, +3, +4, and +5. An element’s resting state can be on levels 0 to +3, while levels +4 and +5 are reserved for user-interacted states such as hover and dragged.
text: Material uses six levels of elevation, each with a corresponding dp value. These values are named for their relative distance above the UI’s surface: 0, +1, +2, +3, +4, and +5. An element’s resting state can be on levels 0 to +3, while levels +4 and +5 are reserved for user-interacted states such as hover and dragged.
